Name: Restaurant 360
Neighborhood: Old Town · on the city walls
Recognition: ★ since 2018 · Michelin Plate before that · seasonal — opens 27 March 2026
What_to_order: the tasting menu only — Chef Marijo Curić's Adriatic-meets-Asia line, ~10 courses
Reservation: Essential — book 2–3 months ahead, weekend dinners go first
Best_time: Dinner; sunset from the wall arches
Notes: Built into the arches above the Old Port — service is exposed to the sea, dinners stop when Jugo blows hard. Closed Nov–March.

Name: Stara Loža (Prijeko Palace)
What_to_order: the chef's day-market menu; oysters from Mali Ston when in season; sea bass in salt crust
Notes: Three spaces stacked vertically — street terrace, 3rd-floor salon, 16-seat rooftop. The one Prijeko Street address that is not a trap.

Name: Proto Fish Restaurant
Recognition: Founded 1886 — oldest operating fish house in Dubrovnik · Michelin Guide recommended
What_to_order: king prawns alla buzara · Ston oysters · grilled sea bass on the bone · crni rižot
Reservation: Book 1 week ahead for the terrace
Best_time: Lunch on the terrace; dinner indoors
Notes: Edward VIII dined here in 1936 with Wallis Simpson on the way to Lokrum — the Esculap group runs it now without breaking the recipe book.

Name: Nautika
Recognition: Set in the former Nautical School (1881); on the city's classic-luxury short list
What_to_order: lobster bisque · whole turbot for two · soufflé glacé Grand Marnier
Reservation: Essential for the sea-side terrace; 2+ weeks
Best_time: Dinner at sunset — Bokar and Lovrijenac frame the view
Notes: The conservative anniversary dinner. Chef Mario Bunda. No experimentation; the room is the experience.

Name: Kopun
Neighborhood: Old Town · top of Jesuit Stairs
Recognition: Michelin Plate · Marin Držić's 16th-c. capon recipe revived
What_to_order: kopun (slow-roasted capon with oranges & honey, the Držić recipe) · šporki makaruli · arancini for dessert
Reservation: Book 5–7 days for the square terrace beside St Ignatius
Best_time: Dinner — the square lights at dusk
Notes: The dishes you cannot order elsewhere. Beside the Boškovićeva (Jesuit) Stairs of Game of Thrones Walk-of-Shame fame.

Name: Konoba Dubrava
Recognition: The reference peka above the city — order 3 h ahead
What_to_order: lamb peka (3 h pre-order) · veal peka · octopus peka · house-made bread
Reservation: Call by 14:00 for an evening peka; 11:00 for lunch
Best_time: Lunch with the Old Town view; sunset from the terrace
Notes: The bell over embers, the right way: salt, rosemary, olive oil, time. Big-sky views down to Lokrum and across the islands.

Name: Bota Šare Mali Ston
Recognition: On-quay oyster bar; runs its own boat trip from the dock
What_to_order: 12 Mali Ston oysters with lemon and Pošip · oyster tempura · sea-bass crudo · mussels na buzaru
Reservation: Walk-in possible at lunch; book dinner Apr–Sep
Best_time: Lunch, then a boat run across the bay to the oyster beds
Notes: The PDO Ostrea edulis at one hour and zero kilometres from the boat. Bring your own bottle of Korčula Pošip if you prefer; corkage is friendly.

Name: Vila Koruna
Recognition: One of the original Mali Ston oyster houses — operating since 1973
What_to_order: oysters au naturel · mussels na buzaru · octopus salad · grilled scampi
Best_time: Lunch off-season; early dinner in summer
Notes: Less polished than Bota Šare, more honest. Rooms above the restaurant if a Pelješac stay is the plan.

Name: Konoba Konavoski Dvori
Recognition: Restored old watermill on the Ljuta — Esculap group · in the Michelin Guide
What_to_order: žaba na brodet (frog brodet) · river trout · lamb peka · asparagus in season
Best_time: Lunch — the cold river runs through the dining room
Notes: Waitstaff in traditional Konavle dress. The frog brodet is one of the dishes you cannot get in the city. The river is the air conditioning.

Name: Konoba Atlantic Kitchen
Recognition: TripAdvisor Travellers' Choice — quiet local reputation since 2017
What_to_order: house-made pappardelle with prawn-walnut sauce · cuttlefish-ink ravioli · grilled tuna
Reservation: Book the small terrace 3–5 days ahead
Best_time: Evening on the Lapad bay side
Notes: When the Old Town is full of cruise traffic, this is where the city's professionals eat. Smaller portions, sharper plating than the konobas inside the walls.

Name: Lady Pi-Pi
Recognition: Cult Old Town grill — no reservations, queue from 17:00
What_to_order: mixed grill platter · grilled squid · house sausages · the lemon-and-oil potatoes
Reservation: Walk-in only — queue starts forming at 17:00
Best_time: Early dinner before the queue, or 21:30+
Notes: Open charcoal in the corner — you eat on a vine-covered terrace within the walls. Cash welcome; cards yes but slow.

Name: Buffet Škola
Recognition: Tiny family sandwich shop · over 50 years on the same lane
What_to_order: homemade-bread sandwich with Drniš pršut and Konavle cheese · domestic glass of red
Best_time: Crew breakfast 09:00–11:00; quick lunch any time
Notes: Bread baked on site. Cash strongly preferred. The pršut comes off Drniš bone; the cheese is the Konavle hard-pressed sheep.

Name: Pizzeria Tabasco
Recognition: Long-running local pizzeria — the antidote to Stradun frozen pizza
What_to_order: Tabasco house pizza (salami, jalapeño, tabasco-honey drizzle) · seafood spaghetti · house tiramisu
Reservation: Walk-in usually fine; book the terrace in July–Aug
Best_time: Late dinner, terrace looks at the lit walls
Notes: Family-friendly, fast, honest. Where local crews eat after service. Pizza is wood-fired, dough is rested overnight.

Name: D'vino Wine Bar
Recognition: The reference Croatian-wine bar of the Old Town · ten consecutive Michelin Guide editions
What_to_order: Plavac Mali flight (Grgić · Miloš · Madirazza · Saints Hills) · Pošip flight · Grk single-pour from Lumbarda
Reservation: Book the cellar table; bar walk-in
Best_time: Aperitivo 18:00–20:00 before a Stara Loža dinner
Notes: Map your Pelješac and Korčula wine list in one sitting. Owner Saša will pour you Grk you cannot find anywhere else in the Old Town.

Name: Glam Café (Beer Therapy)
What_to_order: Garden Brewery IPA · Nova Runda lager · old fashioned with Maraschino float
Best_time: After-dinner, the lane stays lively to 01:00
Notes: The Croatian craft-beer fridge changes weekly. Useful when D'vino next door is full.

Name: Gruž Market (Tržnica Gruž)
Recognition: The provisioning market — closest fresh-fish market to ACI Marina
What_to_order: fish-hall: scorpionfish, sea bream, monkfish, the morning's octopus · outdoor: tomato, fig, mandolins of olive oil, herbs
Notes: Cash only on most stalls. The walk from Gruž ACI Marina is 8 minutes. Mondays are quiet; the rest of the week is the run.

Name: Grgić Vina (cellar door)
Recognition: Founded 1996 by Miljenko "Mike" Grgich — Paris-1976 winemaker's homecoming
What_to_order: Plavac Mali Grgić (the multi-vintage flagship) · Pošip Grgić
Reservation: Email at least 1 week ahead — small, by-appointment tastings
Best_time: Late-morning, then lunch at Bota Šare 50 min away
Notes: Mike Grgich died in 2023; his daughter Violet and nephew Ivo Jeramaz run the estate. Two wines only, every year, no exceptions.

Name: Miloš Stagnum
Recognition: Hugh Johnson called Stagnum "Croatia's first cult wine" · wild yeast, 3 years in old oak
What_to_order: Stagnum (top cuvée, 3 y oak) · Plavac Mali base · Pošip Korčula
Reservation: Visit by appointment only — Frano Miloš personally pours
Best_time: Late afternoon — the 45° slopes catch the last light
Notes: 15 ha across south-facing 150–250 m slopes. The 1993 Stagnum the wine that put Croatia on the international map after the war.
